description: Joint distributions for compound (multi-peril) risk analysis. Correlations derived from empirical monthly co-occurrence 2020-2025.
methodology: Pearson correlation for continuous pairs; contingency matrix for ordinal intensity bands. Prefecture sample = 8 representative units. Matrices are symmetric where applicable.

heat_days_x_crime_rate.interpretation: Higher correlation in summer months (June-August) where heat stress and violent-crime incidence co-vary.

flood_depth_x_displacement_proxy.interpretation: Depth exceeding 2m shifts displacement distribution dramatically toward the 50+ bucket.

resilience_score.notes: Lower = better resilience. Values computed from joint_counts across 47 prefectures.

earthquake_intensity_x_evacuation_capaci.interpretation: Higher shindo events concentrated in lower-capacity zones indicate structural resilience gap above shindo 6-lower.

notes: Matrices are illustrative and derived from a synthesis of empirical co-occurrence; not intended for direct underwriting.
Prefecture sample uses 8 benchmark entities; full 47-prefecture tensors available on request.
Use with tail_risk_metrics.json for CVaR aggregation under compound events.
